semanticCore: Means of approach, seeking access, connection, status, or reach
quranicRelevance: Seeking a means of approach to God (Waseelah - 'ibtanghoo ilayhi al-waseelata').

keyDerivations.meaning: Means of approach, path of access, spiritual connection, high status, or bridge

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَابْتَغُوا إِلَيْهِ الْوَسِيلَةَ (5:35): O you who believe! Fear God and seek a means of approach/access to Him (via righteous deeds).
discardedNoise: Intercession disputes in modern theological schools.
summaryEn: The root represents seeking approach (Tawassul), ordering believers to seek means of spiritual approach (Waseelah) to God through righteous deeds.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الرغبة والتقرب والتوصل بالوسائل الصالحة (وسيلة)، أمراً المؤمنين بالتقرب إلى رضا ربهم بابتغاء العمل الصالح الموصل لرحمته.
rawText: تَوَسَّلَ بِالدَّلْوِ إِلَى المَآءِ 5 [He sought to get at, or obtain, the water by means of the bucket]. (M in art. دلو.) === تَوَسَّلَ إِلَيْهِ بِكَذَا He sought to bring himself near to him, or to approach to him, to gain access to him, or to advance himself in his favour, by such a thing: (Msb, &c.:) so too 🡻وَسَّلَ; (S, K;) and 🡻وَسَلَ, aor. يَسِلُ. (Msb.) وَسِيلَةٌ A means of access to a thing; (IAth;) a means of becoming near to a thing: (IAth, Msb:) these are the primary significations: (IAth:) a means of becoming near to, or intimate with, or of ingratiating oneself with, another: (S:) honourable rank or station with a king: degree: affinity: (K:) a tie, or connexion: (TA:) it may be rendered a means of access, nearness, intimacy, ingratiating oneself, attachment, or connexion: and also, of attainment, or accomplishment. وَسَّلَ 2 see 5. وَسڤلَ 1 see 5.
